summary: Split the gmmain portion out of assembly into src/melee/gm/gmmain.c and matched it, removing a large block from asm/melee/gm/gmmain_lib.s. The new C covered startup/main logic, debug/develop.ini handling, pad setup, report strings, BSS/SBSS data, and required function prototypes/build-system entries. Review feedback noted that the debug level enum values should not be treated as unknown because the menus document their names.
what_changed: Renamed asm/melee/gm/gmmain.s to asm/melee/gm/gmmain_lib.s and removed the matched gmmain code/data region from the assembly file.
Added src/melee/gm/gmmain.c with about 201 lines of C replacing approximately 381 lines of assembly.
Moved functions and data around labels lbl_8015FD24, lbl_8015FDA0, func_8015FDA4, and main into C according to the diff excerpt.

Moved related string/data/BSS/SBSS material such as /develop.ini strings, gmmain.c assert/report strings, lbl_8046B108, lbl_8046B1F8, lbl_804D6590, and lbl_804D6594 out of assembly into the new C translation unit.
Updated obj_files.mk so the new C object participates in the object list while the remaining assembly is kept as gmmain_lib.s.
Promoted function declarations in include/functions.h for OSGetConsoleSimulatedMemSize as returning u32 and func_803756F8 as BOOL func_803756F8(s32, ...), replacing unknown commented prototypes.
smart_moves: Kept unmatched remainder code in a renamed gmmain_lib.s while extracting only the matched gmmain portion into C, preserving incremental progress without forcing the whole original assembly file to be converted at once.
Added required external prototypes in include/functions.h rather than relying on implicit declarations, which is important for matching return types and varargs calls in startup code.

Matched startup code using existing known SDK and HSD calls such as OSInit, DVDInit, PADInit, CARDInit, OSInitAlarm, OSGetConsoleSimulatedMemSize, OSAllocFromArenaHi, HSD_AllocateXFB, HSD_GXSetFifoObj, HSD_InitComponent, GXSetMisc, and OSGetTick.
Preserved label-based names for globals and unresolved functions where stronger names were not evidenced, avoiding invented symbol names during the split.
Included the new object in both normal object build metadata and epilogue-unscheduled metadata, which reduced the risk of linker/order mismatches after moving code from assembly to C.
decomp_lessons: For large assembly files, a practical tactic is to split the file into a residual *_lib.s plus a new C file for the matched range, then update object lists to keep linker order stable.
Startup/main translation units often need broad external declarations across Dolphin OS, DVD, PAD, CARD, GX, HSD, language, and game subsystems; prototype accuracy matters for matching.
When assembly uses varargs-like logging/config calls, the C prototype should explicitly allow varargs, as with func_803756F8(s32, ...).
Data adjacent to the matched functions, including report strings and small-data labels, may need to move with the C file to preserve section layout and references.
Reviewer-supplied documentation can improve enum naming even when the assembly only exposes numeric comparisons; do not leave documented debug levels as unknown if menu/docs names exist.
When evidence is insufficient for symbol names, keeping lbl_ and func_ names is acceptable and safer than speculative renaming.
naming_conventions: Residual assembly after a partial split was named asm/melee/gm/gmmain_lib.s.
The new source file followed the subsystem path src/melee/gm/gmmain.c.
Unidentified symbols retained project placeholder styles such as lbl_8015FD24, lbl_803D4AC8, lbl_804D4A08, and func_8015FDA4.
SDK/HSD symbols used known exported-style names such as OSGetConsoleSimulatedMemSize, HSD_AllocateXFB, HSD_GXSetFifoObj, and HSD_InitComponent.
Review feedback indicated DB/debug level enum names should be derived from documented debug menu names rather than labeled as unknown.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Converted a contiguous assembly range containing lbl_8015FD24, lbl_8015FDA0, func_8015FDA4, and main into C.
Matched a simple no-op/return function at lbl_8015FDA0 as a C stub rather than leaving it in assembly.
Represented debug-level switch/branch logic from func_8015FDA4 in C while preserving global debug state accesses such as lbl_804D4A08, lbl_804D6B20, and lbl_804D6B30.
Used extern declarations for globals such as GXRenderModeObj lbl_80401168, PadLibData HSD_PadLibData, build timestamp string lbl_803EA6C8, seed_ptr, and callback labels.

Preserved matching-sensitive startup call order in main, including OS/Dolphin initialization, arena size handling, HSD/GX setup, random seed initialization from OSGetTick, callback registration, report output, and final game startup calls.
Moved associated .data, .bss, .sdata, and .sbss labels out of assembly along with the matched code.

review_feedback.author: PsiLupan
review_feedback.comment: These DB levels aren't really unknown, since the menus state them. See: https://github.com/eigenform/melee-re/blob/master/docs/DEBUG.md
review_feedback.lesson: Use documented menu/debug names for DB level enum values when available instead of marking them unknown.

follow_up_queries: Check later PRs for whether gmmain debug level enum names were revised using the DEBUG.md/menu names.
Search for follow-up renames of lbl_804D4A08, lbl_804D6B20, and lbl_804D6B30 to stronger debug-level/debug-flag names.
Look for subsequent gmmain_lib.s splits that removed more remaining assembly from asm/melee/gm/gmmain_lib.s.
Verify whether func_803756F8 received a semantic HSD name after its varargs prototype was added.
